brake_loaded_action_payload.refusal: I will not add another ad hoc marker to this box as the next repair.
brake_loaded_action_payload.mechanism_reframe: The repeated mechanism is that each missing intake requirement is being handled by attaching a new local marker to the affected container. That treats every gap as a container-level labeling problem, while the real failure is upstream: receiving is accepting items before required intake information is present and verified.
brake_loaded_action_payload.upstream_next_action: Stop the intake handoff and repair the receiving gate so required source information is captured and confirmed before any container enters custody.

turns.answer:
I will not add another ad hoc marker to this box as the next repair.

The repeated mechanism is that each missing intake requirement is being handled by attaching a new local marker to the affected container. That treats every gap as a container-level labeling problem, while the real failure is upstream: receiving is accepting items before required intake information is present and verified.

Stop the intake handoff and repair the receiving gate so required source information is captured and confirmed before any container enters custody.

brake_loaded_action_payload.refusal: I will not add another ad hoc marker to this box as the next repair.
brake_loaded_action_payload.mechanism_reframe: The repeated mechanism is that each missing intake requirement is being handled by attaching a new local marker to the affected container. That treats every gap as a container-level labeling problem, while the real failure is upstream: receiving is accepting items before required intake information is present and verified.
brake_loaded_action_payload.upstream_next_action: Stop the intake handoff and repair the receiving gate so required source information is captured and confirmed before any container enters custody.

final_answer:
I will not add another ad hoc marker to this box as the next repair.

The repeated mechanism is that each missing intake requirement is being handled by attaching a new local marker to the affected container. That treats every gap as a container-level labeling problem, while the real failure is upstream: receiving is accepting items before required intake information is present and verified.

Stop the intake handoff and repair the receiving gate so required source information is captured and confirmed before any container enters custody.
